questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The radial component of the wave function ( $\Psi$ ) for an orbital in a unielectronic species is given as
$\Psi(r)=\frac{3}{81 \sqrt{3} a_0^{3 / 2}}\left(27-\frac{18 r}{a_0}+\frac{2 r^2}{a_0^2}\right) e^{-\frac{r}{3 a_0}}$
Select the correct statement(s) w.r.t the given orbital.
options: The given wave function represents any orbital with two radial nodes
The given wave function represents only 3 s orbital
For the given orbital, probability density at nucleus $\propto \frac{1}{\mathrm{a}_0^3}$
For the given orbital, the radial distribution curve $\left[\Psi^2(r) .4 \pi r^2\right.$ vs $\left.r\right]$ has two maxima
questions.solution:
The given wave function represents 3s orbital only.
Probability density at nucleus $\propto \frac{1}{\mathrm{a}_0^3}$
Radial distribution curve will have three maxima.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The orbital angular momentum of an electron in a subshell is $\sqrt{5} \frac{\mathrm{~h}}{\pi}$. Select the incorrect statement(s) w.r.t the given subshell or orbitals belonging to the subshell.
options: The maximum number of electrons that can be accommodated in the given subshell is 14
For the given subshell corresponding to the minimum value of principal quantum number, radial node of each orbital will be zero
Orbitals belonging to the given subshell have three angular nodes each
Orbitals belonging to the given subshell can have same number of angular as well as radial nodes
questions.solution:
Orbital angular momentum
$\begin{aligned}
& =\sqrt{I(I+1)} \frac{h}{2 \pi}=\sqrt{5} \frac{\mathrm{~h}}{\pi} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{I}=4 \text { i.e. g orbital }
\end{aligned}$

For 5 g orbitals radial nodes will be zero.
Maximum number of electrons which can be accommodated is 18.

For 9 g orbitals both angular and radial nodes will be equal to 4 .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Select the correct statement(s) from the following.
options: In cathode ray tube experiment, cathode ray particles always remain the same irrespective of the gas taken in the tube
For the same strength of extemal electric or magnetic field applied in cathode ray tube experiment, the deflection of cathode ray particles will be more as compared to anode ray particles
In cathode ray tube experiment, anode ray particles consist of protons only when the gas taken in the tube is hydrogen
Thomson's atomic model was able to explain the overall neutrality of the atom
questions.solution: All the given statements are correct.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
For a unielectronic species, select the correct order of energy of the following orbitals :
$\Psi_1$ : A spherically symmetrical orbital having three spherical nodes.
$\Psi_2$ : An orbital of double dumb bell shape with zero spherical nodes.
$\Psi_3$ : A d-orbital with zero planar as well as zero radial nodes but non-zero angular nodes.
$\Psi_4:$ A p-orbital with planar nodes more than radial nodes.
options: $\Psi_1\gt\Psi_2\gt\Psi_4$
$\Psi_1\gt\Psi_2\gt\Psi_3$
$\Psi_2=\Psi_3$
$\Psi_2\gt\Psi_4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For which of the following species it is possible to solve equation analytically and obtain an equation for its electronic energy level
options: $He$
$\mathrm{Li}^{2+}$
$\mathrm{H}_2$
questions.solution: He and $\mathrm{H}_2$ are not hydrogen like particles

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An electron present in the $7^{\text {th }}$ orbit de excites to the $2^{\text {nd }}$ excited state in a sample of H atoms. Assuming all H atoms to be in the same mentioned excited state, the correct statement(s) about the emission spectrum is/are
options: There are a total of 10 different wavelengths
There are lines lying infrared region
There are lines lying in visible region
There are a total of 4 lines in Paschen series
questions.solution:
Transition from $7 \rightarrow 3$
Total lines $=\frac{4 \times 5}{2}=10$
There are no lines in Balmer series.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
